target.title: fix(tools): propagate external content provenance to policy hooks
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

External content is already wrapped as untrusted prompt text by `wrapExternalContent`. Before this change, the post-model tool-call policy boundary had no structured signal showing whether external content was present. Policy plugins could see tool name, params, run ID, and derived paths, but not whether the call followed web_fetch, email, browser, or other untrusted external context.

Additionally:
- The detector only recognized synthetic `BEGIN>>>` markers, not production `id="...">>>` markers
- Prompt provenance was seeded into a catalog context that normal core tools never received before their first hook call (the context was spread into a copy, so later mutations were invisible to wrapped tools)
- `catalogToolHookContext` was used before its declaration (TDZ), which would throw a ReferenceError at runtime

## What Changed

- **New detector** in `src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.ts`: parses production `<<<EXTERNAL_UNTRUSTED_CONTENT id="...">>>` markers from `wrapExternalContent` output
- **New types** in `src/plugins/hook-types.ts`: `PluginHookExternalContentSource` and `PluginHookExternalContentProvenance`
- **Hook propagation**: `externalContent` flows through `HookContext`, before_tool_call plugin events, and trusted tool policy events. Provenance is refreshed after each tool result
- **Shared hook context fix** in `src/agents/agent-tools.ts`: `createOpenClawCodingTools` mutates `beforeToolCallHookContext` in place (same object reference) instead of spreading into a new object. Wrapped core tools now see prompt-build and current-prompt provenance updates
- **TDZ fix** in `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts`: moved `catalogToolHookContext` declaration before the `toolsRaw` IIFE where it is first used
- **Scanner guard**: `Object.values()` and provenance refresh wrapped in try/catch so hostile proxy objects cannot break tool execution
- **Anti-spoofing**: `Source:` labels only read from the metadata section between the start marker and `---` separator
- **Docs** in `docs/plugins/hooks.md`: documented the new `externalContent` field and source semantics
- **SDK surface budget** in `scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs`: updated defaults to match current export counts after upstream merges

## Evidence

All external content provenance tests pass:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts \
  src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts \
  -t "external content provenance" --reporter=verbose
```

```
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  5 passed | 59 skipped (60)
   Duration  13.72s
```

Spoof test passes:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts \
  src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts \
  -t "ignores spoofed source labels" --reporter=verbose
```

```
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  1 passed | 63 skipped (64)
   Duration  4.91s
```

Plugin SDK surface budget check passes:

```bash
node scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs --check
```

```
(no output, exit 0)
```

Lint and type checks pass:

```bash
npx oxlint src/agents/agent-tools.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs
pnpm run check:test-types
```

```
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
```
target.author: jason-allen-oneal
